description: Lifecycle case (pricing decision) with a reversed decision and a ruled-out/deprecated item; the chat contains the superseded content, the live state does not.

chat.content: How should we price the new analytics add-on?
A simple flat 99 dollars per month per account is easiest to sell.
Two big customers said flat pricing punishes light users and blocks expansion.
Then change it: the decision is usage-based pricing, not the flat 99 per month. Flat pricing is dropped.
Sales claims enterprises only want per-seat pricing. Right?
We ruled that out — the enterprise interviews showed they prefer usage-based too, with a cap. Per-seat is not what they asked for.
Yes: the entry price must stay below Competitor X's 79 dollars per month.
Sales wants an annual commitment discount; finance wants month-to-month to protect revenue recognition. Open.
Whether to offer a free tier at all — undecided. So: usage-based pricing, enterprises want usage not seats, must stay under 79, commitment-discount conflict and free-tier question open.
